states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial state after server connection, awaiting client identification.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: Client has provided a username; server is awaiting a password.

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Successful login; client is authorized to perform file and directory operations.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Client has specified a file to be renamed and is awaiting the target name.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER_INIT
states_list.description: A data transfer (file upload, download, or listing) is being initialized.

invariants_list.rule: PASS must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE, MLSD) require the user to be successfully authenticated (USER and PASS).
A data connection must be established using PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT before executing a transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).
The QUIT command can be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
If authentication fails (e.g., response 530), the client may retry by sending a new USER command.
The RNTO command must be preceded by a RNFR command in the same session.
TYPE.type_code: The value must be 'A' or 'I' (ASCII or Image/Binary).
PORT.address_info: Comma-separated string of 6 integers representing host and port: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2.

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, APPE, CAPI, CDUP, DELE, EPRT, EPSV, FEAT
Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: DATA_TRANSFER_INIT

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: Trace evidence shows response code 331 (password required) following USER command.
Trace evidence shows response code 230 (logged in) following PASS command.
Trace evidence shows response code 530 (login failed) keeping the session in a non-authenticated state.
Trace evidence shows typical data transfer response sequence (150, 226) while remaining authenticated.
Trace evidence shows typical data transfer response sequence (150, 226) while remaining authenticated.
Trace evidence shows typical data transfer response sequence (150, 226) while remaining authenticated.
Trace evidence shows response code 350 (pending further info) following RNFR.
Trace evidence shows response code 250 (action complete) following RNTO.
Trace evidence shows response code 221 (closing connection) following QUIT from authenticated state.
Trace evidence shows response code 221 (closing connection) following QUIT from initial state.
Trace evidence shows response code 250 (action complete) for directory change while remaining authenticated.
Trace evidence shows response code 257 (path created) for print working directory while remaining authenticated.

status_changes.description: PASS must be sent immediately following a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP protocol sequence as documented in the provided spec evidence.
